knightsScenario: Someone ate the Duchess’s cherry tart — and it was one of Aria, Bram, and Cleo.

characters.name: Cleo
statements: Either I ate the tart or Bram and I are the same kind.

explanation: A knight’s statements are always true and a knave’s are always false. Only one full line-up fits every statement — here is the check.
Aria is a knight, so the claim “Either Cleo and I are different kinds or Cleo is a knave” must be true — and it is.

Cleo is a knave, so the claim “Either I ate the tart or Bram and I are the same kind” must be false — and it is.
That pins everything down: Aria = Knight, Bram = Knight, Cleo = Knave. And the one who ate the tart is Bram.
